title: Reading guide: History of the Russian Revolution Vol 2
summary: No single chapter-by-chapter guide exists, but the IMT July Days reading guide + ISR analysis covers Vol 2 well, especially chs 24-26.
body:
No purpose-built study guide for HRR Vol 2 exists, but here's the best stack for chapters 24-26 (July Days):

**Best starting point — IMT July Days reading guide**
https://marxist.com/reading-guide-the-july-days-1917.htm
Maps directly onto chs 24-26. Five sections: background, movement from below, the two days, balance sheet (ch.26), counter-revolution aftermath. Draws on Lenin, Trotsky, Zinoviev, Sukhanov, Alan Woods.

**Best secondary analysis — ISR Part 2 (Amy Muldoon)**
https://isreview.org/issue/60/history-russian-revolution-part-2/index.html
Covers the arc of the whole volume. Good on Trotsky's dialectical method, Kornilov coup, peasant/national movements.

**Ch. 26 specifically — 'Could the Bolsheviks Have Seized Power in July?'**
https://litci.org/en/could-the-bolsheviks-have-seized-the-power-in-july-part-iii/
This chapter is effectively a standalone essay on revolutionary timing. Core argument: power dropped from the government's hands for two days (seizure was physically possible) but the provinces, the front, and much of Petrograd weren't ready — workers still hadn't broken the 'February umbilical cord'. Historical parallels: June Days 1848, Spartacus Week 1919. The party's correct call: participate to limit damage, protect cadres, learn lessons for October.

**Discussion questions (adjacent text, Marxist Student Federation)**
https://marxiststudent.com/the-history-of-the-russian-revolution-to-brest-litovsk-study-guide/
Useful self-test questions: What was the impact of the July offensive? Why did Bolsheviks advocate 'All Power to the Soviets' as a minority? Soviet democracy vs parliamentary democracy?

**Full text free on Marxists.org:**
- Ch 24: https://www.marxists.org/archive/trotsky/1930/hrr/ch24.htm
- Ch 25: https://www.marxists.org/archive/trotsky/1930/hrr/ch25.htm
- Ch 26: https://www.marxists.org/archive/trotsky/1930/hrr/ch26.htm
- Vol 2 PDF: https://www.marxists.org/ebooks/trotsky/history-of-the-russian-revolution/ebook-history-of-the-russian-revolution-v2.pdf
